title: Real-Time PCR as a Gold Standard for High-Sensitivity Pathogen Detection in Food
author: Sophian, Alfi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Real-  time   PCR (  qPCR  ) has   emerged   as   a     cornerstone     technology     for     detecting     foodborne     pathogens     with     unparalleled     sensitivity     and     speed  .   Unlike     traditional     culture-based     methods  ,   qPCR     enables     quantification     of     bacterial  ,   viral  ,   and     parasitic   DNA   or   RNA   within     hours  ,   reducing     response     time     during     food     safety     crises  .   This     rapid     detection     capability     enhances     outbreak     prevention     and     supports     timely     recalls  .   qPCR     is     highly     specific  ,   minimizing     false     positives  ,   and     can     be     multiplexed     to     detect     multiple     pathogens   in   a     single     test  .   Its     integration     into     national     food     safety     systems     strengthens     surveillance  ,   improves     traceability  ,   and     builds     consumer     trust  .   With     proper     implementation  ,   qPCR     can     transform     food     safety     monitoring     into     a     proactive  , data-  driven     process     that     protects     public     health     and     ensures     compliance     with     international     food     trade     standards  .
